beats.reasoning: Aiden heads downtown early to grab a good spot at a bar near Pike Place; timing is tight to make the 11am kickoff, and sunny weather makes the drive pleasant.

Late-night drive home from Capitol Hill; Aiden is energized from socializing and settles in for a wind-down before sleep.

beats.activity: Wind down and sleep at home.

diary:
Monday turned out to be one of those days that reminds me why I love this city. Started with the usual—coffee in Kirkland, then the drive to the office in Tukwila to handle some listing prep and client calls. But honestly, I couldn't focus much. The World Cup match was calling, so I ditched early and drove downtown to catch USA vs Wales at a bar near Pike Place. The energy was electric, and the beer tasted better with a crowd cheering around me.

After lunch, I had this impulse to hit Lake Union for a kayak. The weather was perfect—74 degrees, pure sunshine. Being out on the water, just me and the ripples, felt like exactly what I needed. My shoulders finally relaxed. Showered up at home, then headed to Kremwerk in Capitol Hill to dance with friends. Salsa's been my escape lately, and tonight it delivered. Ended the day tired but genuinely happy.
thought_process: I made a conscious choice today to prioritize living over working. Normally I'd power through the day at the office, but the combination of perfect weather and the World Cup created this window of permission to do something different. The Light Rail closure didn't really affect me since I was driving anyway, but it made me grateful for the flexibility. My mood shifted the moment I decided to abandon the office—there was this guilt at first, then relief. The kayaking was the real turning point though; I needed that solitude to reset. By the time I got to dancing, I was in a completely different headspace. At 54, I'm learning that some days the best business move is stepping away from business entirely.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
